question: Change to Indirect: He said to me, 'Are you coming to the party?'
options: He asked me if I was coming to the party.
He asked me that if I was coming to the party.
He said to me whether I was coming to the party.
He asked me if you were coming to the party.
answer: He asked me if I was coming to the party.
explanation: For Yes/No questions, we use 'if' or 'whether'. The structure changes from question to assertive (Sub + Verb). ✅

question: Change to Indirect: She said, 'Where are you going?'
options: She asked where I was going.
She asked that where I was going.
She asked where was I going.
She said where I was going.
answer: She asked where I was going.
explanation: In Wh- questions, the Wh-word acts as the connector. No 'that' is used. ✅

question: Change to Indirect: I said to him, 'Can you solve this sum?'
options: I asked him if he can solve this sum.
I asked him if he could solve that sum.
I asked him that if he could solve that sum.
I told him if he could solve that sum.
answer: I asked him if he could solve that sum.

question: Change to Indirect: The teacher asked the boy, 'Why are you late?'
options: The teacher asked the boy why was he late.
The teacher asked the boy why he was late.
The teacher asked the boy that why he was late.
The teacher told the boy why he was late.
answer: The teacher asked the boy why he was late.
explanation: After the Wh-word, the subject (he) must come before the verb (was). ✅

question: Change to Indirect: He said, 'I must go now.'
options: He said that he must go now.
He said that he had to go then.
He said that he must go then.
Both B and C are correct.
answer: Both B and C are correct.
explanation: 'Must' can remain 'must' or change to 'had to' in indirect speech, and 'now' changes to 'then'. ✅

question: Change to Indirect: My mother said to me, 'Did you see my keys?'
options: My mother asked me if I had seen her keys.
My mother asked me if I saw her keys.
My mother asked me that if I had seen her keys.
My mother told me if I had seen her keys.
answer: My mother asked me if I had seen her keys.
explanation: Simple Past (Did see) changes to Past Perfect (had seen). ✅

question: Change to Indirect: She said, 'May I use your pen?'
options: She asked if she might use my pen.
She asked if she may use my pen.
She asked that she might use my pen.
She said that she might use my pen.
answer: She asked if she might use my pen.
explanation: 'May' changes to 'might' and 'your' changes to 'my' (assuming the object is 'me'). ✅

question: Change to Indirect: They said to us, 'How do you know this?'
options: They asked us how we knew that.
They asked us how did we know that.
They asked us that how we knew that.
They told us how we knew that.
answer: They asked us how we knew that.
explanation: Simple Present (know) changes to Simple Past (knew). The question structure 'do you' is removed. ✅

question: Change to Indirect: He asked, 'What is the matter?'
options: He asked what was the matter.
He asked what the matter was.
He asked that what the matter was.
He asked what the matter is.
answer: He asked what the matter was.
explanation: In indirect questions, the verb (was) comes at the end of the clause. ✅

question: Change to Indirect: I said to her, 'Will you help me?'
options: I asked her if she will help me.
I asked her if she would help me.
I asked her whether she would help me.
Both B and C are correct.
answer: Both B and C are correct.
explanation: 'Will' changes to 'would' and 'if/whether' can be used interchangeably. ✅
